mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, generating positive forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5 percent signals labor market tightening that accelerates corporate earnings growth, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat in greater than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly lifts corporate revenues and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month acceleration in industrial production during late-cycle expansions triggers inflation expectations and anticipated monetary tightening, producing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, inflationary pressures force the Federal Reserve to halt asset purchases, raising real discount rates and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL greater than 240 coincides with positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which sustains corporate profit growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive revisions in quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales confirm robust aggregate demand, which reduces recession probability and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in GDPC1 and RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.1 million units signal a self-sustaining real estate boom that amplifies financial sector leverage and consumer spending, producing positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: HOUST greater than 1100 is followed by negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of instances.
